program: Travel English -- Europe Without a Translator
hub_subtitle: Travel English for the airport, the check-in counter, the hotel and passport control — so that Europe needs no translator

lesson.menu_desc: Telling the agent what you booked, packed and chose -- and solving three kilos of excess baggage alone -- 35 slides
lesson.subtitle: Lesson 2 -- The Check-In Counter
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Rosângela | Lesson 2 -- The Check-In Counter

listenings.text: Good morning, and welcome to Terminal 3. This is an announcement for all passengers on the flight to Lisbon. Before you come to the counter, please have your passport ready. Here is the information about your bags. Your ticket includes one checked bag of twenty-three kilos, and one small bag with you in the cabin. This is your luggage allowance. If your bag is heavier than twenty-three kilos, this is excess baggage, and there is a fee of one hundred dollars. The fee depends on the weight, and it depends on your ticket, so please ask the agent at the counter. You can also choose your seat at the counter: a window seat, or an aisle seat. Boarding time today is eleven forty, at gate twenty-two. Thank you very much, and have a good flight.

Hello. My name is Marta, and I am in the line for the Lisbon flight, like you. Can I tell you what I did? Last month I booked this flight on the internet, and I chose an aisle seat, because I am tall and I like to stand up and walk. Two days ago I packed my bag, and last night I packed it again, because it was too heavy. My luggage allowance is twenty-three kilos, and my bag was twenty-six. So I took out two books and a pair of shoes, and I put them in my carry-on. This morning I took a taxi, I arrived early, at eight o'clock, and I had a coffee here. I did not pay any fee. My boarding time is eleven forty, so I have two hours. Two hours is a long time, but I am not in a hurry. And you? What did you do?

paras: Last month, Rosângela booked two tickets to Lisbon. She chose the morning flight, because she likes to arrive early and rest at the hotel. On the internet, she chose two seats: a window seat for her husband, and an aisle seat for her. It was easy, and she did not ask anybody for help.

Yesterday she packed her checked bag. She packed three dresses, two pairs of shoes, and a present for her friend in Lisbon. Her husband packed his bag in ten minutes. This morning they took a taxi to the airport, and they arrived at Terminal 3 at nine o'clock.

At the counter, the agent asked for their passports. Then he put Rosângela's bag on the machine. The bag was twenty-six kilos. Her luggage allowance was twenty-three. 'This is excess baggage,' the agent said. 'There is a fee of one hundred dollars, but it depends on the weight.' Rosângela did not pay. She opened her bag, she took out the present and two pairs of shoes, and she put them in her husband's bag. His bag was only eighteen kilos. The second time, her bag was twenty-two kilos. She paid nothing, and then she asked one more question, alone, in English: 'What time is boarding?'

gist.prompt: What is the main idea of the text?
choices: Rosângela paid one hundred dollars for excess baggage, because her bag was too heavy.
Rosângela had a problem with her bag at the counter, and she solved it herself, in English, without paying.
Rosângela's husband packed both bags and talked to the agent for her.

items: Rosângela booked the tickets last month.
Last month, Rosângela booked two tickets to Lisbon.
She chose a window seat for herself.
She chose a window seat for her husband, and an aisle seat for her.
Her bag was heavier than her luggage allowance.
The bag was twenty-six kilos, and her allowance was twenty-three.
She paid the fee for excess baggage.
She did not pay. She took out the present and two pairs of shoes.
Her husband's bag was lighter than her bag.
His bag was only eighteen kilos.

items.situation: "Good morning. Where are you flying today?" Answer, and say who is with you.
tips: Good morning. I am flying to Lisbon, with my husband.

items.situation: "When did you book your flight?" Use a time expression.
tips: I booked it last month, on the internet.

items.situation: "Did you pack this bag yourself?" Answer, and say when.
tips: Yes, I did. I packed it yesterday.
My husband packed his bag himself.

items.situation: "Window or aisle?" Ask for the seat you want, and say what your husband wants.
tips: I would like an aisle seat, please.
My husband would like a window seat.

items.situation: "Your bag is twenty-six kilos. Your allowance is twenty-three." Ask about the fee before you decide.
tips: Is there a problem with my bag? How much is the fee?
What does the fee depend on?

items.situation: You do not want to pay one hundred dollars. Ask the agent for a minute, and say what you are going to do.
tips: Could I have a minute, please?
I opened my bag and I took out two pairs of shoes.

items.situation: Everything is done. Ask the two last questions before you leave the counter.
tips: Could I have my boarding pass?
What time is boarding, and which gate?
